canonical_definition: A property of a model's learned distribution to concentrate its probability mass on a few high-reward or high-preference outputs, rather than covering all possible responses.
papers: On a Connection Between Imitation Learning and RLHF
SimPER: A Minimalist Approach to Preference  Alignment without Hyperparameters

relationships.evidence: “SimPER also theoretically promotes mode-seeking behavior by optimizing the Total Variation distance (TVD) between the model distribution πθ and the distribution of chosen response.”
